content.problemStatement:
Chef loves research! Now he is looking for subarray of maximal length with non-zero product.

Chef has an array **A** with **N** elements: **A1**, **A2**, ..., **AN**. 


Subarray **Aij** of array **A** is elements from index **i** to index **j**: **Ai**, **Ai+1**, ..., **Aj**. 

Product of subarray **Aij** is product of all its elements (from **ith** to **jth**).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer N, denoting the number of elements in the array. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN, denoting the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: A single integer - the maximal length of subarray with non-zero product.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The subarray {2, 3} has a non-zero product and is the longest such subarray.
There are no subarrays with non-zero product.
The subarray {1} or {1} has a non-zero product and is the longest such subarray.

solution.approach: We will use a sliding window approach to find the maximum length of subarray with non-zero product. We will maintain two pointers, start and end, representing the current window. We will also keep track of the product of the elements in the current window. If the product becomes zero, we will move the start pointer to the right until the product becomes non-zero again.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) - where N is the number of elements in the array. We are scanning the array once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) - we are using a constant amount of space to store the pointers and the product.

code.Python3:
import sys
N = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
max_len = 0
for i in range(N):
    prod = 1
    for j in range(i, N):
        prod *= arr[j]
        if prod != 0:
            max_len = max(max_len, j - i + 1)
print(max_len)
